author: catwith1hat
content: <@586161934425128960> This was flagged in my repo as divergence for   Gloas https://github.com/catwith1hat/beacon-breaker/tree/master/items/067#lodestar

author: catwith1hat
content: <@586161934425128960> I am not sure if Lodestar is at fault. If I read the [conclusions](https://github.com/catwith1hat/beacon-breaker/tree/master/items/067#conclusion) correctly (at the end of the item), all the other clients (or rather the spec) have a supply inflation bug: "Lodestar's caching arguably fixes a supply-asymmetry bug in the spec's literal semantics: spec mints pre_balance + queue_drain_total to the EL (via the queue + sweep withdrawal emissions) while only decrementing pre_balance on the CL — a net +queue_drain_total supply inflation. Lodestar's behavior preserves the supply invariant. However, lodestar diverges from 5 spec-conformant clients, which is a worse outcome for consensus stability."
